metadata.name: int_iterable__list_user_unnest
metadata.comment: Table that unnests the `email_list_id` field for each user to create a new record for each user and the unique list it was associated with at a unique update. Due to the unnesting functions varying greatly between warehouses, conditional logic is applied throughout the model to appropriately accommodate the model being ran within Redshift, Snowflake, BigQuery, and Postgres.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.comment: Table that joins the message type and channel records together into one comprehensive model to be referenced downstream.

metadata.name: iterable__events
metadata.comment: Table that provides a comprehensive view of all events with enriched information such as the campaign, user effected, and template used.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.comment: Unique id of the event generated by Fivetran

unique_event_id.name: unique_event_id
unique_event_id.comment: A unique surrogate key of event_id, _fivetran_user_id, and source_relation.

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for the new Iterable schema in Aug 2023, for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: Reference to the campaign from which the event originated

content_id.name: content_id
content_id.comment: Reference to the content the event is associated with

created_on.name: created_on
created_on.comment: Day when the event was created

email.name: email
email.comment: The user's email. User identifier, for email-based projects. Previously was the unique identifier for user records, for the old Iterable schema before the August 2023 updates.

additional_properties.name: additional_properties
additional_properties.comment: json object containing addition event properties

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.comment: Name provided to the individual event

message_bus_id.name: message_bus_id
message_bus_id.comment: Reference to the message bus associated with the event

message_id.name: message_id
message_id.comment: Reference to the message the event is associated with

message_type_id.name: message_type_id
message_type_id.comment: Reference to the type of message the event is associated with

recipient_state.name: recipient_state
recipient_state.comment: The state of the recipient upon receiving the event

transactional_data.name: transactional_data
transactional_data.comment: Transactional data associated with the event

user_agent.name: user_agent
user_agent.comment: User agent associated with the event

user_agent_device.name: user_agent_device
user_agent_device.comment: The device of the user agent

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when the event record was synced by Fivetran

is_campaign_recurring.name: is_campaign_recurring
is_campaign_recurring.comment: Boolean indicating if the campaign is recurrent

recurring_campaign_name.name: recurring_campaign_name
recurring_campaign_name.comment: The name of the recurring campaign (if applicable)

recurring_campaign_id.name: recurring_campaign_id
recurring_campaign_id.comment: The campaign if of the recurring campaign (if applicable)

is_app_already_running.name: is_app_already_running
is_app_already_running.comment: Boolean indicating if the app is already running

catalog_collection_count.name: catalog_collection_count
catalog_collection_count.comment: The count of products or items collected in a catalog associated with the event.

catalog_lookup_count.name: catalog_lookup_count
catalog_lookup_count.comment: The count of times a catalog was looked up or queried during the event.

canonical_url_id.name: canonical_url_id
canonical_url_id.comment: Reference to the url associated with the event

device.name: device
device.comment: The device associated with the event

email_id.name: email_id
email_id.comment: Reference to the email associated with the event

email_subject.name: email_subject
email_subject.comment: Subject of the email associated with the event

experiment_id.name: experiment_id
experiment_id.comment: Reference to the experiment used if the event is an experiment

from_phone_number_id.name: from_phone_number_id
from_phone_number_id.comment: Reference to the phone number which the event is from

from_smssender_id.name: from_smssender_id
from_smssender_id.comment: Reference to the sms sender which the event is from

link_id.name: link_id
link_id.comment: Reference to the link associated with the event

link_url.name: link_url
link_url.comment: Url of the link associated with the event

platform_endpoint.name: platform_endpoint
platform_endpoint.comment: The specific platform endpoint of the event

push_message.name: push_message
push_message.comment: Boolean indicating if the event is a push message

sms_message.name: sms_message
sms_message.comment: Boolean indicating if the event is an sms message

to_phone_number.name: to_phone_number
to_phone_number.comment: To phone number associated with the event

workflow_id.name: workflow_id
workflow_id.comment: Reference to the workflow which the event originated

workflow_name.name: workflow_name
workflow_name.comment: Name of the workflow which the event originated

city.name: city
city.comment: The city associated with the event, which could be the location of the recipient.

clicked_url.name: clicked_url
clicked_url.comment: The URL that was clicked by the recipient in response to the marketing message.

country.name: country
country.comment: The country associated with the event, which could be the location of the recipient.

error_code.name: error_code
error_code.comment: The error code or message indicating any issues or errors encountered during the event.

expires_at.name: expires_at
expires_at.comment: The date and time when the event or message is set to expire or become invalid.

from_phone_number.name: from_phone_number
from_phone_number.comment: The phone number from which the SMS message was sent.

in_app_body.name: in_app_body
in_app_body.comment: The content or body of an in-app message that was sent as part of the event.

is_sms_estimation.name: is_sms_estimation
is_sms_estimation.comment: A boolean indicating whether the SMS message is an estimation or not.

labels.name: labels
labels.comment: Any labels or tags associated with the event, which can be used for categorization or organization.

message_status.name: message_status
message_status.comment: The status of the marketing message, such as sent, delivered, opened, etc.

mms_send_count.name: mms_send_count
mms_send_count.comment: The count of multimedia messages (MMS) sent as part of the event.

reason.name: reason
reason.comment: The reason or cause associated with a specific event or action.

sms_send_count.name: sms_send_count
sms_send_count.comment: The count of SMS messages sent as part of the event.

template_name.name: template_name
template_name.comment: User defined name of the template

template_creator_user_id.name: template_creator_user_id
template_creator_user_id.comment: Reference to the user who created the template

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: iterable__list_user_history
metadata.comment: Table that recreates the `list_user_history` source table. Additionally, this table provides an `is_current` field to identify the current lists associated with the user. The table coalesces list_id values from both the direct list_user relationships and the unnested email_list_ids from user history to ensure comprehensive list coverage. The grain of this table is at the user/list/updated_at level.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for the new Iterable schema in Aug 2023, for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type.

user_email.name: user_email
user_email.comment: Email of the user if it exists.

user_signup_date.name: user_signup_date
user_signup_date.comment: Timestamp of when the user signed up

user_signup_source.name: user_signup_source
user_signup_source.comment: Source where the user signed up

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The list associated with the user. Uses coalesced values prioritizing `list_id` from `LIST_USER` over the unnested `email_list_ids` from `USER_HISTORY` for comprehensive list coverage.

is_current.name: is_current
is_current.comment: Boolean indicating if the current list is active

list_name.name: list_name
list_name.comment: Name of the list associated with the user

list_type.name: list_type
list_type.comment: Type of list associated with the user

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: iterable__user_campaign
metadata.comment: Table that provides total and unique metrics for a user associated with particular campaigns and if available, experiments.

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for the new Iterable schema in Aug 2023, for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The unique identifier of the campaign which the user metrics are associated

experiment_id.name: experiment_id
experiment_id.comment: The unique experiment variation of the campaign

user_email.name: user_email
user_email.comment: Email of the user if it exists.

user_full_name.name: user_full_name
user_full_name.comment: The users first and last name

campaign_name.name: campaign_name
campaign_name.comment: The name of the campaign which the user metrics are associated

template_id.name: template_id
template_id.comment: The unique identifier of the template used within the campaign

template_name.name: template_name
template_name.comment: The name of the template used for the campaign

recurring_campaign_id.name: recurring_campaign_id
recurring_campaign_id.comment: The unique identifier of the recurring campaign which the campaign is tied to

recurring_campaign_name.name: recurring_campaign_name
recurring_campaign_name.comment: The name of the recurring campaign which the campaign is tied to

first_event_at.name: first_event_at
first_event_at.comment: Timestamp of the first campaign/experiment event associated with the user

last_event_at.name: last_event_at
last_event_at.comment: Timestamp of the most recent campaign/experiment event associated with the user

first_open_or_click_event_at.name: first_open_or_click_event_at
first_open_or_click_event_at.comment: The time of the first open or click time. This is the first time a user interacts with a campaign, recording the earliest occurring event out of 'emailOpen', 'emailClick', and 'pushOpen'.

total_emailclick.name: total_emailclick
total_emailclick.comment: Total email clicks events the user took within the campaign/experiment

total_emailunsubscribe.name: total_emailunsubscribe
total_emailunsubscribe.comment: Total email unsubscription events the user performed within the campaign/experiment

total_emailcomplaint.name: total_emailcomplaint
total_emailcomplaint.comment: Total email complaint events the user performed within the campaign/experiment

total_customevent.name: total_customevent
total_customevent.comment: Total custom events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_emailsubscribe.name: total_emailsubscribe
total_emailsubscribe.comment: Total email subscription events associate with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_emailopen.name: total_emailopen
total_emailopen.comment: Total email open events the user performed within the campaign/experiment

total_pushsend.name: total_pushsend
total_pushsend.comment: Total push send events the user performed within the campaign/experiment

total_smsbounce.name: total_smsbounce
total_smsbounce.comment: Total sms bounce events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_pushbounce.name: total_pushbounce
total_pushbounce.comment: Total push bounce events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_inappsendskip.name: total_inappsendskip
total_inappsendskip.comment: Total in app send skip events the user performed within the campaign/experiment

total_smssend.name: total_smssend
total_smssend.comment: Total sms send events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_inappsend.name: total_inappsend
total_inappsend.comment: Total in app send events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_pushopen.name: total_pushopen
total_pushopen.comment: Total push open events the user performed within the campaign/experiment

total_emailsend.name: total_emailsend
total_emailsend.comment: Total email send event associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_pushsendskip.name: total_pushsendskip
total_pushsendskip.comment: Total push send skip events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_inappopen.name: total_inappopen
total_inappopen.comment: Total in app open events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_emailsendskip.name: total_emailsendskip
total_emailsendskip.comment: Total email send skip events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_emailbounce.name: total_emailbounce
total_emailbounce.comment: Total email bounce events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_inappclick.name: total_inappclick
total_inappclick.comment: Total in app click events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

total_pushuninstall.name: total_pushuninstall
total_pushuninstall.comment: Total push uninstall events associated with the user within the campaign/experiment

unique_user_campaign_id.name: unique_user_campaign_id
unique_user_campaign_id.comment: Unique ID combining source_relation, unique_user_key, campaign_id, and if available, experiment_id.

metadata.name: iterable__user_unsubscriptions
metadata.comment: Table that generates the total channel and message types a user has chosen to unsubscribe.

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for the new Iterable schema in Aug 2023, for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

channel_id.name: channel_id
channel_id.comment: Unique identifier of the channel which the user is unsubscribed

message_type_id.name: message_type_id
message_type_id.comment: Unique identifier of the message type which the user is unsubscribed

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the user unsubscribed from the channel or message type

channel_name.name: channel_name
channel_name.comment: The name of the channel which the user is unsubscribed

message_type_name.name: message_type_name
message_type_name.comment: The name of the message type which the user is unsubscribed

channel_type.name: channel_type
channel_type.comment: The channel type which the user is unsubscribed

message_medium.name: message_medium
message_medium.comment: The medium type the message was provided which the user is unsubscribed

is_unsubscribed_channel_wide.name: is_unsubscribed_channel_wide
is_unsubscribed_channel_wide.comment: Boolean indicating if the user unsubscribed at the channel level (i.e. unsubscribed to all message types) or if they unsubscribed from only an individual message type.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: iterable__users
metadata.comment: Table that provides unique user enriched information.

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for the new Iterable schema in Aug 2023, for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

email.name: email
email.comment: The user's email. User identifier, for email-based projects. Previously was the unique identifier for user records, for the old Iterable schema before the August 2023 updates.

signup_date.name: signup_date
signup_date.comment: Timestamp of when the user signed up

signup_source.name: signup_source
signup_source.comment: Source where the user signed up

phone_number.name: phone_number
phone_number.comment: Phone number associated with the user

email_list_ids.name: email_list_ids
email_list_ids.comment: Array of all email lists associated with the user, derived from the list_user table relationships

count_lists.name: count_lists
count_lists.comment: Total count of lists associated with the user

count_total_campaigns.name: count_total_campaigns
count_total_campaigns.comment: Count of all campaigns the user has been in

first_event_at.name: first_event_at
first_event_at.comment: The first event the user ever encountered

last_event_at.name: last_event_at
last_event_at.comment: The most recent event the user has encountered

total_emailclick.name: total_emailclick
total_emailclick.comment: Total number of email clicks the user has performed

total_emailunsubscribe.name: total_emailunsubscribe
total_emailunsubscribe.comment: Total unsubscription events the user has performed

total_emailcomplaint.name: total_emailcomplaint
total_emailcomplaint.comment: Total email complaints the user has performed

total_customevent.name: total_customevent
total_customevent.comment: Total custom events associated with the user

total_emailsubscribe.name: total_emailsubscribe
total_emailsubscribe.comment: Total subscription events the user has performed

total_emailopen.name: total_emailopen
total_emailopen.comment: Total email opened events the user has performed

total_pushsend.name: total_pushsend
total_pushsend.comment: The push send events the user has experienced

total_smsbounce.name: total_smsbounce
total_smsbounce.comment: Total sms bounces that are associated with the user

total_pushbounce.name: total_pushbounce
total_pushbounce.comment: Total number of push bounces associated with the user

total_inappsendskip.name: total_inappsendskip
total_inappsendskip.comment: Total in app send skips associated with the user

total_smssend.name: total_smssend
total_smssend.comment: Total sms sends that the user has experienced

total_inappsend.name: total_inappsend
total_inappsend.comment: Total in app sends the user has experienced

total_pushopen.name: total_pushopen
total_pushopen.comment: Total push messages the user has opened

total_emailsend.name: total_emailsend
total_emailsend.comment: Total emails that have been sent to the user

total_pushsendskip.name: total_pushsendskip
total_pushsendskip.comment: Total push sends the user has skipped

total_inappopen.name: total_inappopen
total_inappopen.comment: Total in app opens the user has performed

total_emailsendskip.name: total_emailsendskip
total_emailsendskip.comment: Total email sends the user has skipped

total_emailbounce.name: total_emailbounce
total_emailbounce.comment: Total number of emails that have bounced from the user

total_inappclick.name: total_inappclick
total_inappclick.comment: Total in app clicks associated with the user

total_pushuninstall.name: total_pushuninstall
total_pushuninstall.comment: Total push uninstall events the user has performed

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.comment: Table containing a history of campaigns.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: Unique identifier of the campaign record

campaign_name.name: campaign_name
campaign_name.comment: User defined name of the campaign.

campaign_state.name: campaign_state
campaign_state.comment: State of the campaign. Can be Draft, Ready, Scheduled, Running, Finished, Starting, Aborted or Recurring

campaign_type.name: campaign_type
campaign_type.comment: The campaign type. Can be Blast or Triggered

send_size.name: send_size
send_size.comment: Size of the campaign. Number of individuals included in the campaign

message_medium.name: message_medium
message_medium.comment: The medium that this message was sent via, for example Email or InApp

recurring_campaign_id.name: recurring_campaign_id
recurring_campaign_id.comment: Reference to the recurring campaign, if applicable

created_by_user_id.name: created_by_user_id
created_by_user_id.comment: Reference to the user who created the campaign

updated_by_user_id.name: updated_by_user_id
updated_by_user_id.comment: ID of the user who updated this record

workflow_id.name: workflow_id
workflow_id.comment: Reference to the workflow which the event originated

metadata.name: stg_iterable__event
metadata.comment: Table containing individual events within campaigns.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier derived from hashing campaign_id, created_at, and event_name.

unique_event_id.name: unique_event_id
unique_event_id.comment: A surrogate key made up of event_id, _fivetran_user_id, and source_relation.

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type. This exists for users of the new schema starting Aug 2023. In the old schema, the unique identifier is email.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: Reference to the campaign from which the event originated

content_id.name: content_id
content_id.comment: Reference to the content the event is associated with

email.name: email
email.comment: Reference to the unique user the event was applied to

additional_properties.name: additional_properties
additional_properties.comment: json object containing addition event properties

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.comment: Name provided to the individual event

message_bus_id.name: message_bus_id
message_bus_id.comment: Reference to the message bus associated with the event

message_id.name: message_id
message_id.comment: Reference to the message the event is associated with

message_type_id.name: message_type_id
message_type_id.comment: Reference to the type of message the event is associated with

recipient_state.name: recipient_state
recipient_state.comment: The state of the recipient upon receiving the event

transactional_data.name: transactional_data
transactional_data.comment: Transactional data associated with the event

user_agent.name: user_agent
user_agent.comment: User agent associated with the event

user_agent_device.name: user_agent_device
user_agent_device.comment: The device of the user agent

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The time when a record was last updated by Fivetran.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_iterable__event_extension
metadata.comment: Table containing addition properties from the event table.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier derived from hashing campaign_id, created_at, and event_name.

unique_event_id.name: unique_event_id
unique_event_id.comment: A surrogate key made up of event_id, _fivetran_user_id, and source_relation.

is_app_already_running.name: is_app_already_running
is_app_already_running.comment: Boolean indicating if the app is currently running or not

catalog_collection_count.name: catalog_collection_count
catalog_collection_count.comment: The count of products or items collected in a catalog associated with the event.

catalog_lookup_count.name: catalog_lookup_count
catalog_lookup_count.comment: The count of times a catalog was looked up or queried during the event.

canonical_url_id.name: canonical_url_id
canonical_url_id.comment: Reference to the url associated with the event

content_id.name: content_id
content_id.comment: Reference to the content associated with the event

device.name: device
device.comment: The device associated with the event

email_id.name: email_id
email_id.comment: Reference to the email associated with the event

email_subject.name: email_subject
email_subject.comment: Subject of the email associated with the event

experiment_id.name: experiment_id
experiment_id.comment: Reference to the experiment used if the event is an experiment

from_phone_number_id.name: from_phone_number_id
from_phone_number_id.comment: Reference to the phone number which the event is from

from_smssender_id.name: from_smssender_id
from_smssender_id.comment: Reference to the sms sender which the event is from

link_id.name: link_id
link_id.comment: Reference to the link associated with the event

link_url.name: link_url
link_url.comment: Url of the link associated with the event

platform_endpoint.name: platform_endpoint
platform_endpoint.comment: The specific platform endpoint of the event

push_message.name: push_message
push_message.comment: Boolean indicating if the event is a push message

sms_message.name: sms_message
sms_message.comment: Boolean indicating if the event is an sms message

to_phone_number.name: to_phone_number
to_phone_number.comment: To phone number associated with the event

workflow_id.name: workflow_id
workflow_id.comment: Reference to the workflow which the event originated

workflow_name.name: workflow_name
workflow_name.comment: Name of the workflow which the event originated

city.name: city
city.comment: The city associated with the event, which could be the location of the recipient.

clicked_url.name: clicked_url
clicked_url.comment: The URL that was clicked by the recipient in response to the marketing message.

country.name: country
country.comment: The country associated with the event, which could be the location of the recipient.

error_code.name: error_code
error_code.comment: The error code or message indicating any issues or errors encountered during the event.

expires_at.name: expires_at
expires_at.comment: The date and time when the event or message is set to expire or become invalid.

from_phone_number.name: from_phone_number
from_phone_number.comment: The phone number from which the SMS message was sent.

in_app_body.name: in_app_body
in_app_body.comment: The content or body of an in-app message that was sent as part of the event.

is_sms_estimation.name: is_sms_estimation
is_sms_estimation.comment: A boolean indicating whether the SMS message is an estimation or not.

labels.name: labels
labels.comment: Any labels or tags associated with the event, which can be used for categorization or organization.

message_status.name: message_status
message_status.comment: The status of the marketing message, such as sent, delivered, opened, etc.

mms_send_count.name: mms_send_count
mms_send_count.comment: The count of multimedia messages (MMS) sent as part of the event.

reason.name: reason
reason.comment: The reason or cause associated with a specific event or action.

sms_send_count.name: sms_send_count
sms_send_count.comment: The count of SMS messages sent as part of the event.

message_type_name.comment: User defined name of the message type

channel_id.name: channel_id
channel_id.comment: Channel that this message type belongs to

message_type_created_at.name: message_type_created_at
message_type_created_at.comment: Time when the message type was initially created

frequency_cap.name: frequency_cap
frequency_cap.comment: The maximum number of times a message of this type can be sent to a recipient within a specified time period to prevent over-messaging

rate_limit_per_minute.name: rate_limit_per_minute
rate_limit_per_minute.comment: The maximum number of messages of this type that can be sent to recipients in a minute, ensuring controlled message delivery

subscription_policy.name: subscription_policy
subscription_policy.comment: Information about the policy or rules governing how users can subscribe to or unsubscribe from this specific message type, like OptOut

message_type_updated_at.name: message_type_updated_at
message_type_updated_at.comment: Time when the message type was last updated or modified

_fivetran_user_id.name: _fivetran_user_id
_fivetran_user_id.comment: A Fivetran-created unique identifier for users, derived from hashing user_id and/or email, depending on project type. This exists for users of the new schema starting Aug 2023. In the old schema, the unique identifier is email.

unique_user_key.name: unique_user_key
unique_user_key.comment: The unique identifier of the user. With the new schema (Aug 2023), it is the _fivetran_user_id. This is a hash of user_id and/or email, depending on project type. If using the old schema, it is email.

unsub_message_type_unique_key.name: unsub_message_type_unique_key
unsub_message_type_unique_key.comment: A surrogate key providing a unique key for each record. If using user_unsubscribed_message_type_history, it uses '_fivetran_id', 'email', 'message_type_id', 'updated_at', and 'source_relation'. If not using the history table, it uses '_fivetran_id', 'message_type_id', and 'source_relation'.
